skill.name: rowan
skill.description: Cloud-based quantum chemistry platform providing a Python API for computational chemistry workflows including pKa prediction, geometry optimization, conformational search, molecular property calculations, protein-ligand docking, and AI protein cofolding. Provides cloud computing resources without local installation.
skill.summary: Cloud-based quantum chemistry platform accessed through a Python API. Run DFT, semi-empirical, and neural network calculations with no local compute cluster required.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 620 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES located in markdown documentation code examples. This skill contains no executable scripts (.py, .sh, or binary files). The SKILL.md and 6 reference markdown files contain Python code examples inside markdown code fences that demonstrate the Rowan API. The static analyzer misidentified code block backticks as Ruby shell backtick execution (505 instances) and flagged API documentation patterns as credential access and network risks. The Rowan platform legitimately requires API keys and internet access for cloud-based quantum chemistry calculations. No prompt injection or malicious intent was detected.

medium_findings.title: External commands in markdown code examples (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 505 instances of Ruby/shell backtick execution across SKILL.md and 6 reference markdown files. All instances are Python code examples inside markdown code fences (```python). This skill contains zero executable scripts. The backtick characters are markdown syntax for code blocks, not shell execution operators.

medium_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm in code examples (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithm references across multiple files. These are references to computational chemistry method names (e.g., B3LYP, PBE, GFN-xTB) in documentation, not cryptographic algorithm usage. These are quantum chemistry method names from the Rowan platform.

medium_findings.title: Dangerous behavior combination heuristic (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged a heuristic dangerous combination of code execution + network + credential access. The combination exists because the skill documents the Rowan Python API which legitimately requires API keys for cloud-based quantum chemistry calculations. The skill itself contains no executable code.

low_findings.title: C2 keywords in chemical computing code examples (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged C2 keywords in molecule_handling.md and rdkit_native.md. These are legitimate computational chemistry terms (e.g., molecule_lookup, batch functions) in documentation code examples, not command-and-control indicators.

low_findings.title: Environment variable access in documentation (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged ROWAN_API_KEY access at 7 locations. These are in documentation showing users how to authenticate with the Rowan cloud platform. The skill recommends using environment variables (best practice) rather than hardcoding keys.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs to legitimate service (Informational)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 7 hardcoded URLs. These are links to the Rowan platform website and documentation at labs.rowansci.com and docs.rowansci.com. These are legitimate service URLs for a commercial quantum chemistry platform.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance in workflow monitoring code (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged system reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md and reference files. These are calls to rowan.whoami() and workflow.status checks, which are standard Rowan API calls for authentication verification and workflow monitoring.

content.user_title: Run Quantum Chemistry Calculations with Rowan
content.value_statement: Computational chemistry requires specialized software and significant computing resources. This skill enables you to run quantum chemistry calculations, molecular simulations, and protein-ligand docking through a unified cloud API with no local setup needed.
actual_capabilities: Run pKa prediction, geometry optimization, and conformational search workflows through the Rowan cloud API
Perform protein-ligand docking with AutoDock Vina using automated cloud execution
Predict protein structures using AI cofolding models including Chai-1 and Boltz
Calculate molecular properties such as solubility, redox potential, and molecular descriptors
Create molecules from SMILES strings, XYZ coordinates, RDKit objects, and file formats
Manage batch processing workflows and organize projects with folders
limitations: Requires an active Rowan API key and internet connection for all calculations
Cannot execute workflows without available cloud computing credits in the Rowan account
Skill provides documentation and workflows only, no executable scripts are included
Long-running calculations may be subject to API timeout and rate limit restrictions

use_cases.title: Drug Discovery Property Prediction
use_cases.description: Run pKa, solubility, and redox potential predictions across compound libraries to prioritize synthesis candidates based on calculated molecular properties.

use_cases.title: Protein-Ligand Docking and Structure Prediction
use_cases.description: Dock small molecules into protein targets and predict complex structures using AI cofolding models for drug discovery projects.

use_cases.title: Molecular Geometry Optimization
use_cases.description: Optimize molecular geometries and search conformational space to find stable drug candidate structures for further analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Calculate pKa for a Molecule
prompt_templates.prompt: Predict the pKa value for phenol (SMILES: c1ccccc1O) using the Rowan quantum chemistry skill. Set up the molecule, submit a pKa calculation workflow, wait for completion, and report the strongest acid pKa value with units.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher needs to predict the acidity of a drug molecule to assess its bioavailability at different pH levels.

prompt_templates.title: Run Virtual Screening Docking
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Rowan skill to perform molecular docking of the provided SMILES compound library against the EGFR kinase protein (PDB ID: 1M17). Define the binding pocket and submit docking workflows. Report the top 5 docking scores.
prompt_templates.scenario: A drug discovery scientist wants to dock a library of compounds against a protein target to identify potential lead molecules.

prompt_templates.title: Predict Protein-Ligand Complex with AI Cofolding
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the Rowan skill, predict the structure of a protein-ligand complex using the Chai-1 AI model. Submit a protein cofolding workflow with the given amino acid sequence and ligand SMILES. Report the predicted TM score and interface pTM.
prompt_templates.scenario: A structural biologist needs to predict a protein-ligand complex structure for understanding binding mode and interactions.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Property Calculation and Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a batch property prediction pipeline using the Rowan skill. Process the provided list of SMILES strings through pKa calculation workflows. Organize results in project folders, handle errors for invalid molecules, and produce a summary table with calculated pKa values for successful calculations.
prompt_templates.scenario: A computational chemist needs to process multiple molecules in parallel and organize results for downstream analysis.

output_examples.input: Calculate pKa for the molecule aspirin (SMILES: CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O)
Dock compound library against EGFR kinase (PDB: 1M17)
Predict protein-ligand complex structure using Chai-1 cofolding

best_practices: Use environment variables to store your Rowan API key instead of hardcoding it in scripts for better security
Organize related calculations into project folders for easier tracking, retrieval, and batch management
Check workflow status and handle errors with try-catch blocks before accessing result data to prevent runtime failures
anti_patterns: Embedding API keys directly in source code instead of using environment variables or secret management tools
Submitting workflows without checking credit balance, which leads to unexpected interruptions during calculations
Ignoring workflow status checks and attempting to access results from failed or still-running workflows

faq.question: What is Rowan?
faq.answer: Rowan is a cloud-based quantum chemistry platform that provides a Python API for running computational chemistry calculations without local hardware.

faq.question: Do I need to install any software?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install the rowan-python package using pip or uv. All calculations run on Rowan cloud servers, not on your local machine.

faq.question: How do I get an API key?
faq.answer: Generate an API key from the Rowan web interface at labs.rowansci.com and set it as the ROWAN_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: What calculation methods are supported?
faq.answer: Rowan supports DFT methods (B3LYP, PBE), semi-empirical methods (GFN-xTB), and neural network potentials (AIMNet2 and Egret).

faq.question: What molecule formats are accepted?
faq.answer: Rowan accepts SMILES strings, XYZ coordinates, RDKit molecule objects, and common file formats through the stjames library.

faq.question: How does credit usage and billing work?
faq.answer: Each workflow consumes credits based on calculation complexity. Check your balance with the whoami() function and set max_credits limits per workflow.
